title: MIGRANT SEASONAL HEAD START - 211info
text: CONCORDIA CENTER MIGRANT SEASONAL HEAD START Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Migrant and Seasonal Head Start programs provide education, health, nutrition, social services, and additional support, including developmental screening and is available for migrant children while their parents work in the fields. The program helps preschoolers develop the language, reading, and math skills they need to be successful in school. Parental involvement is emphasized. Services for parents and pregnant women include parenting classes, family support, and leadership opportunities. Wrap-around child care may also be available. 4611 Lancaster Drive NE Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: Migrant and Seasonal Head Start must be 3-5 years old, and have parents who are seasonal or migrant workers that have moved from another geographical location within the last 2 years. Family income must be 100% federal poverty level and come primarily from agricultural labor. Hours: Hours vary Email (503) 485-0899 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 485-0899 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Marion County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Lancaster Drive NE and Hayesville Road. Adjacent to Redeemer Lutheran Church. Services: Early Head Start * Migrants Head Start * Migrants If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. CONCORDIA CENTER
